query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "eva-bench: a new end-to-end framework for evaluating voice agents" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on energy efficiency and resource consumption of agentic frameworks using Small Language Models (SLMs), which is a distinct construct from the user's question regarding the "EVA-Bench" framework for evaluating voice agents. While both involve "agentic frameworks," the specific domain (software engineering issue resolution vs. voice agent evaluation) and the primary variables (energy/token usage vs. voice interaction capabilities) do not satisfy the criteria for same-mechanism e
